ifab-law11-offside-p103.extracted_text: A player is in an offside position if: any part of the head, body or feet is in the opponents' half (excluding the halfway line) and any part of the head, body or feet is nearer to the opponents' goal line than both the ball and the second-last opponent.

ifab-law12-dogso-handball-p118.extracted_text: Where a player denies the opposing team a goal or an obvious goal-scoring opportunity by committing a deliberate handball offence the player is sent off whatever the position of the offence.
ifab-law12-dogso-handball-p118.topic: denying a goal by deliberate handball (DOGSO)
